summary: A symmetrical, raised rectangular pavilion with a stepped central roof and four tall corner towers with overhanging flat caps. The main body is two visible stories tall with horizontal banding of lighter slabs and rows of small windows; the stepped roof uses alternating dark and light layers. Corner towers are brick-faced with vertical stonebrick (or similar) corner pillars and decorative iron bars or fence details. A row of white slab/blocks forms a perimeter base around the building.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main hall with four corner towers; central stepped roof rising from main hall
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical on both axes (square/centered symmetry)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: decorative vertical details on towers (could be iron bars)

elements: four corner towers with overhang caps
horizontal light slab bands across facades
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint roughly 21x21; raise walls to ~6-7 blocks high for main hall before roof steps.
Place four square towers at each corner, each ~3x3 footprint, reaching higher (~14-16 blocks) with stonebrick corner columns and brick faces.
Create a stepped pyramid roof centered on the hall: alternate dark slab rows and light slab rows decreasing inward to a small flat top.
Add horizontal lighter slab bands (one block thick) wrapping mid-height of the walls and vertical white pillars on facades as shown.
Line the base perimeter with white slabs/blocks spaced continuously; add small 1x1 windows in repeating pattern between bands.
Top each tower with a flat wide cap made of stone slabs edged with lighter slabs and a dark center (nether-like) and add a single glow block at cap center.
uncertainties: Exact block types for dark roof layers: appear as dark slabs (possibly netherbrick slabs) — not certain.
Decorative vertical elements on tower faces might be iron bars, chains, or fences; images are small so identification is uncertain.
Interior layout and number of interior floors/rooms cannot be determined from exterior views.
Precise footprint dimensions inferred from block counting in images; could be ±2 blocks.
